What is the difference between denaturing and renaturing?
Chemistry of Nucleic Acid.answer: Denaturing involves the separation of DNA strands by raising the temperatures above 100⁰c while renaturing involves joining of complementary strands by slowly cooling

Mention three differences between RNA and DNA.
Chemistry of Nucleic Acid.answer:
1. At the carbon no. 2 of the Pentose sugar DNA has only Hydrogen group while RNA has -OH group.

2. Thymine is unique to DNA while uracil is unique to RNA

3. DNA is double strap nded while RNA is single stranded.

Chemistry of Nucleic Acid.question: What is the difference between Nucleotide and nucleoside?
Chemistry of Nucleic Acid.answer:
Sugar + base = nucleoside.

Sugar + base + phosphate group = nucleotide

DNA Replication & Gene Transmission.question: What are the three patterns of DNA replication?

'Both strands of DNA still remain
together after producing daughter strands.' This occurs in what pattern of DNA replication?

'Each parental strand acts as
template for a new strand and remains with it.' This occurs in what pattern of DNA replication. ?
'Both strands break along their length, act as template for a short length of new strands, and then rejoin.' What pattern of DNA replication is this ?
Which one is the right model?
The Meselson & Stahl Experiment to Show the SemiConservative Nature of DNA Replication
DNA Replication & Gene Transmission.answer:
i.E.coli cells were grown in 15NH4Cl as the sole N
source for many generations (15N is a heavy isotope
of the normal 14N). All cells had 15N in their DNA.

ii.Cells were then grown in 14NH4Cl so future generations would use 14N to form their DNA.

iii.Cells were collected after 1 generation & lysed.

iv.Liberated DNA was placed in cesium chloride (CsCl)
density gradient ultracentrifugation. This can separate “heavy” DNA (15N) from “light” DNA (14N).

v.After spinning, generation 1 DNA settled at a density gradient ½ way b/w that of 14 N & 15N DNAs indicating that all generation 1 DNA was a hybrid of
14N & 15N strands.

vi. This 1st result ruled out the conservative model which would have had 2 bands—at the 14 N & 15N gradients—instead of 1.

vii.DNA from the 2nd generation resulted in 2 bands—at the ½ way mark, & and at the 14 N grad.

viii.This result ruled out the dispersive model which would still have had 1 band in the half-way mark.

ix.The dispersive model was further disproved when generation 1 DNA was 1st denatured b/4 being centrifuged.

x.2 bands—14 N & 15N density gradients—emerged instead of the supposed 1 grad at the ½ way mark.

History Review of Genetics.question: What are the four concepts of Genetics developed by Mendel?
History Review of Genetics.answer:
a) Concept of Dominance
b) Concept of Unit Characters
c) Concept of Segregation
d) Concept of Independent Assortment

History Review of Genetics.question: What mendelian concept states that some hereditary traits are dominant over others?

What mendelian concept states that hereditary characteristics were
controlled by discrete of separate factors (now called genes) that occur in pairs, one factor
from each parent?

What concept of mendel states that contrasting factors for a given trait (eg, yellow and greenfor colour traits) in an individual segregate (separate) when gametes are formed, and any single gamete receives only one factor for the trait?

The 2 contrasting genes for a trait are called ____. When both alleles are identical, the organism is said to be ____ for that trait.
When not identical, the organism is ____. In any organism, the gamete is ____ (that is, has half the number of chromosomes) and contains only one of the two chromosomespresent in the parent, and the selection of chromosomes is purely random.

What concept of Mendel concluded that factors for one trait segregate independently of the factors for another
trait?
What are the four limitations to the mendelian system?
History Review of Genetics.answer:
1. Incomplete Dominance .
2. Multiple Allele Inheritance.
3. Sex-linked Inheritance.
4. Polygene/ Multiple gene Inheritance.

History Review of Genetics.question: What mendel limitation occurs when when two a red flower is crossed with a white flower but they were both expressed, thus resulting in the formation of pink flowers?
History Review of Genetics.answer:
Incomplete Dominance

Sickle cell anaemia heterozygotes (AS) is also a kind of incomplete dominance. The blood of
carriers have both the AA and SS haemoglobin.

History Review of Genetics.question: What mendel limitation occurs when some traits like the ABO blood types in humans have 3 possible alleles instead of 2?

There are some genes found on the X chromosome which are not on the Y chromosome. Those genes found only on the X chromosome are said to be Xlinked, and those appearing on the Y chromosome are said to be Y-linked. An example is colour blindness. The gene is X-linked. When a male inherits an X-linked trait, its expression is never masked because there is no corresponding allele on his Y-chromosome. In contrast, females
must have 2 X-linked recessive alleles to express colour blindness, and as a result, there are very few colour blind females as opposed to colour blind males. This is a limitation of mendelian system referred to as ____

A situation whereby many genes act together to produce a phenotype. Skin colour, for instance, is controlled by 3 separate inherited genes, each existing in 2 allelic forms. These are the A.B, and C. the A and B confer dark skin pigment and their effect is additive. The a, b and
c alleles confer light skin tone. Hence, an individual with AABBCC will be very dark.
This is a limitation to the mendelian system referred to as ____
Mendel Concept apply only to diploid organisms and cannot explain the genetics of haploid organisms such as bacteria. True/False

Evidence that DNA might be the main genetic material first emerged from studies on ____ bacteria by in 1928. Griffith was working with ____, a bacterium which owes its virulence to the presence of a ____.
History Review of Genetics.answer: pneumonia-causing, Fredrick Griffith, Streptococcus pneumoniae, polysaccharide capsule
